trade_log.reasoning: QCOM is down 6.9% from entry at $251.02 and is the worst performer in the long book by a margin. In a STRONG_BULL tape where semis broadly are ripping — NVDA +3.6%, AVGO +2.8% — QCOM's inability to participate is a loud signal. The stock is sitting at $233.69, which is $13 above the stop at $220.90 but trending toward it with no visible catalyst to reverse. This is not a pullback in an uptrend; it's relative weakness in a sector that has tailwinds. Rotating out of the laggard and reclaiming $2,103 in capital to redeploy into higher-conviction ideas is the right call here.

INTC is down 3.0% from entry at $115.33, currently at $111.86 — another semi that's failing to track the sector's bull move. With NVDA breaking out and MRVL at $217, INTC is structurally disadvantaged and the position is just dead weight. The stop is at $101.49, which means there's another 9.3% of downside risk still on the table if the name rolls. That's an asymmetric loser in a book that needs to rebuild from -46%. Cutting this frees $782 and eliminates a tail risk position.

OKTA is the standout winner in the long book at +13.7%, entry $123.05, current $139.90. The stop has already been trailed up to $123.67, which is essentially breakeven-plus. Trimming 3 of 8 shares locks in roughly $50/share of realized gain on those lots — approximately $500 in cash — while leaving 5 shares to run with the trailing stop. At 11:54 ET near the close of the morning session, taking some chips off a 13%+ winner in a position that's approaching prior resistance levels is disciplined risk management, not timidity. The remaining 5-share position still participates in any continuation.

MSFT at $460.84 is notable by its absence from this book given a 5/5 bull regime. The stock is mega-cap quality, has AI/cloud exposure that's directly in the market's current thematic lane, and at 10 shares represents a $4,608 notional — under 1% of portfolio, well within the 10% max. Stop set at 12% trailing ($405.54). This is a core blue-chip addition that reduces the book's tilt toward more volatile semis and fills a gaping hole in mega-cap tech exposure. The bull regime fully supports this entry.

META at $616.19 is the most glaring hole in this book given the regime. Large-cap mega-tech with dominant ad revenue, AI monetization kicking in via Advantage+, and the tape is screaming risk-on. 8 shares at $616.19 = $4,929.52, roughly 0.9% of portfolio — undersized intentionally because we're filling slot 12 of 12 on long-term, but this gets us exposure to the name. Stop at $542.25 is 12% trailing from entry, well below any meaningful technical support. At a 5/5 bull regime with SPY printing $757 vs SMA50 of $706, leaving this name completely absent from the book is an active underweight I can't justify.

IONQ is sitting at $70.71 against a stop of $68.47 and TP of $80.72 — the breakout signal is still intact and we're only -1.9% on the position. Adding 6 shares brings total to 20 shares at blended ~$71.13. The risk from current price to stop is $2.24/share, reward to TP is $10.01/share — that's a 4.5:1 R/R on the add. Quantum computing names have shown tape strength in strong bull regimes and this setup hasn't been violated. Adding on no deterioration of thesis with stop already defined is sound risk management, not speculation.

RGTI is up 7.4% from entry at $24.40, sitting 4.3% below the $27.33 take profit. The speculative signal on this name is a low-conviction quantum play — we captured the bulk of the move and the risk/reward from here is asymmetric to the downside. Locking the gain frees a swing slot for higher-quality setups in this bull tape rather than squeezing the last dollar out of a speculative micro-cap.

AMZN at $256.78 is a quality large-cap that belongs in this book in a STRONG_BULL regime. 4 shares = $1,027, well within position sizing. Stop at $243.94 (5% below entry), TP at $287.59 (12% above entry), giving a clean 2.4:1 R/R. AWS cycle acceleration, Prime advertising ramp, and broader tech momentum via QQQ at $741 all support the setup. Not chasing — this is a measured add to the swing book at an orderly entry, not a breakout chase.

The tape is as clean as it gets — SPY $757 above both moving averages, VIX sub-16, QQQ $742 with a 50-day gap of $86, and a 20-day return of 5% — this is a fully confirmed STRONG_BULL and the regime read alone gives you permission to be net-long and leaning in. The problem is the book itself: we're sitting at -46% cumulative with over 93% of capital in cash, which tells you we've been burned by this portfolio's history and the priority isn't getting aggressive, it's getting right. The key action today is pruning the underperformers — QCOM and INTC are both down in a tape where semis are printing all-time highs, and that divergence is information; rotating out of losers in a winner's sector and redeploying into MSFT, which is conspicuously absent from a book that should own it, is the cleaner trade. OKTA at +13.7% is the single best position we have and trimming 3 shares here is not a conviction call against the name — it's basic discipline, locking in gains on the best winner while the trailing stop continues to work on the remainder. The risk I'm watching most closely is RGTI at $26.11 with TP at $27.33 — that's 4.7% away and could print in the next session, so I need to be ready to execute the exit rather than let a winner turn. Into the next cycle, I want to see whether WELL and KO can stabilize or whether the bull tape's rotation out of defensives accelerates — if those positions continue bleeding in a 5/5 bull environment, that's the clearest signal they don't belong here.
agent_decision_log.risk_notes: 1) RGTI at $26.11 is approaching its TP of $27.33 — that's only $1.22 away (4.7%), so be ready to exit or trail next cycle. 2) IONQ is at -0.8% but the stop is $68.47 against current $71.51, so $3.04 of room — watch for continuation or cut it loose if it can't reclaim $72.07 entry. 3) WELL at $200.26 is -3.3% from entry $207.12 with stop at $182.27 — healthcare REITs are a headwind in a bull tape; this is worth monitoring for continued relative weakness. 4) KO at $78.67 vs entry $80.41 (-2.2%) and PG at $140.00 vs entry $143.38 (-2.4%) are both defensive names underperforming in a risk-on tape — both are still stops-away from hard exit but deserve a close look next cycle. 5) SPY position at 12 shares/$757 with a stop at $666.35 is a 12% drawdown buffer — in a STRONG_BULL with RSI at 68, that stop is loose by design but a -12% SPY move would trigger broad book damage simultaneously.

agent_decision_log.commentary: The tape is as clean as it gets — SPY at $757, 7.1% above its 50-day, VIX pinned at 15.9, and every regime indicator flashing green — yet this book is sitting 92.8% in cash with a 46% cumulative loss staring back at us. That disconnect is the central problem this cycle. The P&L drag isn't coming from bad positions; OKTA is up 13.2%, NVDA +3.4%, AVGO +2.5% — the book's losers like WELL (-3.3%) and PG (-2.2%) are noise, not thesis breaks. The real damage was done in prior cycles through either poor entries or premature exits that kept us sidelined during what has been a sustained bull run. This cycle I'm putting two actions to work: initiating META as the final long-term slot — it's inexcusable to run a tech-heavy book in a 5/5 regime without ad-tech/AI exposure there — and adding to IONQ on the swing book where the R/R at current levels is 4.5:1 with the stop already defined at $68.47. OKTA is the one name I need to actively manage — stop at $123.67 is stale given we're at $139.25; I'm flagging that for a trail to approximately $122-123 to protect the 13.2% gain, which is the best performing position in the book. The single risk I'm watching most closely is whether WELL cracks — at $200.36 against a stop of $182.27, a continued healthcare sector rotation lower could take that out, and I'd rather see it hold the $195 level or I'll consider trimming before the stop activates. Into the next cycle, RGTI is within 6% of its $27.33 target — I'll be ready to close that swing and recycle the capital.
agent_decision_log.risk_notes: 1) BOOK DRAWDOWN: Portfolio is down 46% from $1M start — this is the dominant risk. At $539K with 92.8% cash, we've been severely under-invested in what is now a 5/5 bull regime, which is itself the primary risk driver. 2) WELL at $200.36 vs entry $207.12 is -3.3% and stop at $182.27 — healthcare REIT in a rate-sensitive environment; watching closely. 3) OKTA stop at $123.67 is BELOW entry of $123.05, meaning stop needs to be trailed up — current price $139.25 puts the 12% trailing stop at $122.54, but we should be managing this to lock in the 13.2% gain; stop should be raised to approximately $122.54 (12% below current high). 4) IONQ stop at $68.47 is tight — a single down day of 3.2% triggers it. 5) SPY position: 12 shares at $756.69 with stop at $666.35 is a 12% drawdown allowance on index — fine structurally but the stop is very wide. 6) Correlation risk: NVDA, AVGO, MSFT, META (new) are all highly correlated to AI/semis theme — four names in same factor bucket. 7) RGTI approaching TP of $27.33 from $25.69 — only $1.64 away, approximately 6.4% upside to target.

agent_decision_log.commentary: The tape is as clean as it gets right now — SPY pinned above both moving averages, RSI at 68.9 suggesting momentum without being dangerously overbought, and VIX at 16 telling you the options market isn't pricing fear. In this environment, the active question isn't whether to be long, it's whether we're long the right names and sized correctly given the hole we're climbing out of. The book has two things working for it: AVGO up 8.2% and NVDA up 6.4% are doing the heavy lifting, and OKTA at +8.1% is quietly one of our best setups — that stop at $123.67 is just below entry so we're essentially in a free-carry position. On the action side, I'm closing RGTI here — we caught a clean 7.4% on a speculative quantum name, the thesis was short-term, and this tape has better opportunities than grinding out the last 4 points on a thin name; recycling that capital and slot into AMZN as a swing, which gives us a better-quality momentum vehicle with a 2.4:1 setup. The risk I'm watching hardest is the IONQ cluster — 20 shares across two entries both stopping out at $68.47 means a single print through that level hits us twice, and I want to see it hold $69 on any intraday weakness to feel comfortable it's not rolling over. Into next week, I'm focused on whether MSFT can reclaim $460 and whether META stabilizes above $600 — if either of those two breaks materially lower, they're candidates for a trim despite the bullish macro backdrop, because dead weight in a bull market is still dead weight.
agent_decision_log.risk_notes: Book is down 46% from starting capital — the single most critical risk flag is portfolio-level drawdown. Current equity of $539k versus $1M start means we need roughly 85% gains to recover, which demands disciplined risk management, not heroics. Concentration risk: IONQ has two separate entries (14 + 6 shares, total 20 shares at $70.71 avg) in quantum computing — if IONQ breaks $68.47 stop, we take a double hit simultaneously; watching that level closely. META at $601.53 and MSFT at $450.97 are both 2%+ underwater and approaching ranges where thesis needs reconfirmation. WELL at -4.3% ($198.26 vs entry $207.12) is the weakest long-term name in the book — trailing stop at $182.27 is still 8% away but the negative drift is notable. SPY stop at $666.44 is extremely wide (12% trailing) — that's appropriate for a core long-term position but represents meaningful drawdown risk if regime shifts. No earnings catalysts flagged intraday but NVDA earnings cycle always a risk given our 51-share position.
